lessons.title: Verb Tenses Overview
lessons.description: Tell present from past from future without thinking — pick the right verb form by spotting time signals.
lessons.rule: Each tense pairs with signal words. Spot the signal, pick the form.

tldr.rule: Signal word → tense. 'Yesterday' = past, 'since' = present perfect, 'tomorrow' = future.
tldr.example: I saw her yesterday. I have known her since 2020.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Tôi gặp cô ấy hôm qua. Tôi biết cô ấy từ năm 2020.

scenarios.why: Past actions with a clear when → simple past.
scenarios.english: I had pho for lunch yesterday.
scenarios.vietnamese: Hôm qua tôi ăn phở trưa.

scenarios.context: 💼 Saying how long you've worked somewhere
scenarios.why: Started in past, still true now → present perfect with 'for'/'since'.
scenarios.english: I've worked at this company for three years.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi đã làm ở công ty này được ba năm.

scenarios.why: Future arrangement → 'will' for decisions, 'going to' for plans.
scenarios.english: I'll text you when I arrive.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi sẽ nhắn khi đến nơi.

scenarios.why: Habits/repeated actions → simple present.
scenarios.english: She drinks coffee every morning.
scenarios.vietnamese: Cô ấy uống cà phê mỗi sáng.

quickChecks.question: She ___ to Hanoi last summer.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Last summer' is a finished past time → simple past 'went'.

quickChecks.question: I ___ him since 2019.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Since' signals a state that started in the past and continues → present perfect.

quickChecks.question: Tomorrow we ___ to the new cafe.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Tomorrow' → future. After 'will', use the base form ('go'), not '-ing'.

quickChecks.question: Water ___ at 100°C.
quickChecks.explanation: Universal facts → simple present. Subject 'water' (singular) → 'boils'.

sections.heading: Simple Present — habits & facts
sections.body: For routines, repeated actions, and universal truths.
bullets: Signals: every day, usually, always, often, sometimes
3rd person singular (he/she/it) takes -s/-es

examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy uống cà phê mỗi sáng.

sections.heading: Simple Past — finished, dated
sections.body: Action completed at a specific time in the past.
bullets: Signals: yesterday, last week, ago, in 2020
Negative uses 'didn't + base verb' (NOT past form)

examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã đi Huế mùa hè năm ngoái.

examples.note: Negative: didn't + see, NOT didn't + saw.

sections.heading: Present Perfect — past affecting now
sections.body: Bridges past and present: experience up to now, or actions that started before and continue.
bullets: Signals: since, for, already, yet, just, ever, never
have/has + V3 (past participle)

examples.english: I have lived in Hanoi for 5 years.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã sống ở Hà Nội 5 năm (vẫn còn).

sections.heading: Simple Future — predictions, decisions
sections.body: For things that haven't happened yet.
bullets: Signals: tomorrow, next week, soon
will + base verb (no 'to', no -ing)

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt không chia động từ → nhớ thêm -s ở 'he/she/it' và đổi V → V2 cho quá khứ.
'Yesterday', 'last…', 'ago' luôn đi với simple past, KHÔNG đi với present perfect.
Sau 'will' luôn là động từ nguyên thể, không bao giờ V2 hay -ing.

commonMistakes.explanation: 'Yesterday' = thời điểm xác định → simple past.
Sau 'will' luôn dùng V nguyên thể.

practice.vietnamese: Tôi đã ăn tối với gia đình tuần trước.
practice.english: I had dinner with my family last week.

practice.vietnamese: Cô ấy đã sống ở đây từ năm 2020.
Ngày mai tôi sẽ học tiếng Anh.

lessons.title: Articles: a, an, the
lessons.description: Master a / an / the in 5 minutes — the #1 mistake for Vietnamese learners.
lessons.rule: 'a/an' = unknown one. 'the' = the specific one we both know. No article for general plurals.
examples: I bought a book. The book is great.

tldr.rule: First mention → a/an. Second mention or unique thing → the. General plural → nothing.
tldr.example: I saw a cat. The cat was orange. I love cats.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Tôi thấy một con mèo. Con mèo đó màu cam. Tôi thích mèo.

scenarios.why: Naming your job/role = singular countable → needs 'a/an'.
scenarios.english: I'm a software engineer.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi là kỹ sư phần mềm.

scenarios.why: Something general from the menu → 'a/an' for one item.
scenarios.english: I'll have a coffee and the chicken sandwich.
scenarios.vietnamese: Cho tôi một cà phê và món bánh mì gà.

scenarios.why: Most country names take no article. The Netherlands / The UK are exceptions.
scenarios.english: I live in Vietnam, but I've visited the UK.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi sống ở Việt Nam, nhưng đã đi Anh.

scenarios.why: Plural noun used in general sense → no article.
scenarios.english: I love reading books.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi thích đọc sách.

quickChecks.question: She is ___ honest person.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Honest' starts with a vowel SOUND (h is silent) → 'an'.

quickChecks.question: I love ___ music.
quickChecks.explanation: General love for music in general → no article. 'The music' = a specific song/genre.

quickChecks.question: We saw ___ amazing show. ___ show was incredible.
quickChecks.explanation: First time → 'an' (vowel sound). Second mention → 'the' (now specific).

quickChecks.question: He is ___ university student.
quickChecks.explanation: 'University' starts with /ju:/ — a consonant sound — so use 'a', not 'an'.

sections.heading: a vs an — by SOUND, not letter
sections.body: Use 'an' before a vowel sound; 'a' before a consonant sound. The rule is about pronunciation.

sections.heading: When to use 'the'
sections.body: When the thing is specific or already known to both speakers.
bullets: Already mentioned (I saw a dog. The dog was…)
Unique (the sun, the moon, the Earth)
Superlatives & ordinals (the best, the first)

sections.heading: No article — general plurals & uncountables
sections.body: When talking about something in general, drop the article.

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt không có mạo từ → người Việt hay quên 'a/an/the'. Phản xạ: danh từ đếm được số ít LUÔN cần mạo từ.
Lần đầu nhắc → a/an. Nhắc lại → the. Quy tắc nhanh.

commonMistakes.explanation: 'Honest' bắt đầu bằng âm nguyên âm.

practice.vietnamese: Cô ấy là một giáo viên.
Mặt trời mọc ở hướng đông.
practice.english: The sun rises in the east.

practice.vietnamese: Tôi thích đọc sách.

lessons.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
lessons.description: Singular subject = singular verb. Sounds easy, but 'everyone', 'a lot of', 'there is/are' all hide traps.
lessons.rule: Match the verb to the subject. Singular → adds -s. Plural → no -s.

tldr.rule: He / she / it / singular noun → verb + s. 'Everyone' counts as singular.
tldr.example: Everyone is here. The team plays well.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Mọi người đều ở đây. Đội bóng chơi tốt.

scenarios.why: Collective nouns (team, family, staff) are usually singular in American English.
scenarios.english: The team is winning.
scenarios.vietnamese: Đội đang thắng.

scenarios.context: 🙋 Describing what people in your office do
scenarios.why: Plural subject → plural verb (no -s).
scenarios.english: My colleagues work remotely.
scenarios.vietnamese: Đồng nghiệp tôi làm việc từ xa.

scenarios.why: 'Everyone' looks plural but is grammatically singular.
scenarios.english: Everyone loves Friday.
scenarios.vietnamese: Ai cũng yêu thứ Sáu.

scenarios.context: 📋 Saying how many things there are
scenarios.why: 'There is' for 1; 'There are' for 2+ or 'a lot of [plural]'.
scenarios.english: There are five people in the meeting.
scenarios.vietnamese: Có 5 người trong cuộc họp.

quickChecks.question: She ___ to the gym every day.
quickChecks.explanation: Subject 'she' (3rd person singular) → verb + es.

quickChecks.question: Everyone in the room ___ excited.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Everyone' is singular even though it feels plural.

quickChecks.question: There ___ many cars on the road today.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Cars' is plural → 'there are'.

quickChecks.question: My family ___ from Hue.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Family' is a collective noun, treated as singular here.

sections.heading: Basic rule — add -s at 3rd person singular
sections.body: In simple present, he/she/it + verb takes -s/-es.
bullets: I / you / we / they → base verb
he / she / it / singular noun → verb + s/es
Verbs ending in -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o: add -es (watches, goes)

sections.heading: Indefinite pronouns — always singular
sections.body: Words that feel plural but grammatically count as one: everyone, somebody, each, neither, either.

sections.heading: 'There is' vs 'There are'
sections.body: Match the verb to the noun right after it.

examples.english: There is a book on the table.
There are many books on the table.
There is some water in the bottle.

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt không chia động từ. Phản xạ: thấy 'he/she/it' → thêm -s.
'Everyone/everybody' luôn đi với động từ số ít — đừng dịch theo nghĩa.

commonMistakes.wrong: He go to work by bus.
commonMistakes.right: He goes to work by bus.

practice.vietnamese: Cô ấy thường thức dậy lúc 6 giờ.
Có rất nhiều ô tô trên đường.
practice.english: There are many cars on the street.

practice.vietnamese: Mỗi học sinh đều có một quyển sách.

lessons.title: Prepositions: in / on / at
lessons.description: Time and place prepositions follow a 'big to small' logic. Master 3 prepositions, cover 80% of cases.
lessons.rule: Big container → IN. Surface or date → ON. Specific point → AT.
examples: on Monday / on the table / on May 14
at 5 PM / at home / at the door

tldr.rule: Time/place from big to small: IN (year/month/country) → ON (day/surface) → AT (clock time/exact point).
tldr.example: I'll meet you at 5 PM on Friday in May.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Tôi sẽ gặp bạn lúc 5 giờ chiều thứ Sáu vào tháng Năm.

scenarios.why: Pick by precision: clock time → at, day → on, larger period → in.
scenarios.english: Class starts at 9 on Monday in September.
scenarios.vietnamese: Lớp bắt đầu lúc 9 giờ thứ Hai vào tháng Chín.

scenarios.why: Inside a place → in. At a point → at. On a surface → on.
scenarios.english: I'm at the cafe in District 1, sitting on the terrace.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi đang ở quán cà phê trong quận 1, ngồi trên ban công.

scenarios.why: Big vehicles → on. Small/private → in.
scenarios.english: I'm on the bus, but my friend is in a taxi.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi đang trên xe buýt, còn bạn tôi trong taxi.

scenarios.why: Specific date → on. Month only → in.
scenarios.english: My birthday is on May 14 — in May, basically.
scenarios.vietnamese: Sinh nhật tôi vào 14 tháng Năm — đại loại là tháng Năm.

quickChecks.question: I was born ___ May 14.
quickChecks.explanation: Specific date → 'on'.

quickChecks.question: I'll see you ___ 5 PM.
quickChecks.explanation: Exact clock time → 'at'.

quickChecks.question: She is ___ the kitchen.
quickChecks.explanation: Inside a space → 'in'.

quickChecks.question: I commute ___ the bus.
quickChecks.explanation: Public transport (bus, train, plane) → 'on'. Cars/taxis → 'in'.

sections.heading: Time — big to small
sections.body: Move from large period to single point: IN year/month → ON day → AT clock time.
bullets: IN: century, decade, year, month, season, parts of day (in the morning)
ON: specific day, date, holiday
AT: clock time, night, weekend

sections.heading: Place — same big-to-small logic
sections.body: IN inside a space, ON a surface, AT a precise point.

examples.english: The book is on the shelf.
I'll meet you at the cafe.

sections.heading: Exceptions to memorize
sections.body: A few cases break the pattern. Learn them as set phrases.
bullets: at night, in the morning/afternoon/evening
on time = punctual, in time = early enough
at home / at school / at work

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt dùng 'ở/vào/lúc' cho mọi thứ — tiếng Anh phân tay 3 giới từ riêng.
Phương tiện: 'in a car/taxi' nhưng 'on a bus/plane/train'.

practice.vietnamese: Tôi sinh năm 1995.
Cuộc họp vào thứ Hai lúc 9 giờ sáng.
practice.english: The meeting is on Monday at 9 AM.

practice.vietnamese: Anh ấy đang ở nhà.

lessons.title: Plural Nouns & Uncountables
lessons.description: When to add -s, and which 'logical-feeling-plural' words are actually uncountable.
lessons.rule: Countable + > 1 → add -s/-es. Uncountable (information, advice, water) → never -s.

tldr.rule: Many things → +s. But 'advice', 'information', 'news' are uncountable — say 'a piece of advice'.
tldr.example: She gave me three pieces of advice and lots of useful information.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Cô ấy cho tôi 3 lời khuyên và nhiều thông tin hữu ích.

scenarios.why: Counting items → add -s.
scenarios.english: I bought three books and two pens.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi mua 3 quyển sách và 2 cây bút.

scenarios.why: 'Advice' is uncountable — no 'an advice'.
scenarios.english: Can I get some advice?
scenarios.vietnamese: Cho tôi xin lời khuyên được không?

scenarios.why: 'News' ends in -s but is singular → 'is', not 'are'.
scenarios.english: The news is shocking today.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tin tức hôm nay gây sốc.

scenarios.why: Irregular plural — child → children.
scenarios.english: My two children play piano.
scenarios.vietnamese: Hai con tôi chơi piano.

quickChecks.question: I have two ___.
quickChecks.explanation: Regular plural → just add -s.

quickChecks.question: She gave me ___ advice.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Advice' is uncountable — use 'some', 'a lot of', or 'a piece of'.

quickChecks.question: Three ___ are playing.
quickChecks.explanation: Irregular plural: child → children.

quickChecks.question: The news ___ shocking.
quickChecks.explanation: 'News' looks plural but acts singular.

sections.heading: Regular plurals
sections.body: Add -s for most nouns. Spelling shifts for endings -s/-sh/-ch/-x/-o (+es) and consonant + y (y → ies).
bullets: book → books, dog → dogs
box → boxes, watch → watches
baby → babies
Irregulars: man/men, woman/women, child/children, foot/feet, tooth/teeth, person/people

sections.heading: Common uncountable nouns
sections.body: No -s, no a/an, takes singular verb. Count them with units.
bullets: Liquids/materials: water, milk, rice
Abstract: information, advice, news, work, homework
Units: a glass of water, a piece of advice, a slice of bread

sections.heading: False plurals — end in -s but singular
sections.body: Don't be fooled by the spelling.

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt không có hình thức số nhiều — người Việt thường quên thêm -s.
'Information', 'advice', 'news' KHÔNG đếm được — đừng nói 'an advice'.

commonMistakes.wrong: She gave me a good advice.

practice.vietnamese: Tôi có ba quyển sách.
Cô ấy cho tôi nhiều lời khuyên.
practice.english: She gave me a lot of advice.

practice.vietnamese: Hai đứa trẻ đang chơi.

lessons.title: Pronouns: I, me, my, mine
lessons.description: Subject vs object pronouns, possessive forms, and reflexive — pick the right one in one second.
lessons.rule: Before verb → I, he, she. After verb/preposition → me, him, her. Possessive → my (before noun), mine (alone).
examples: This is my book = This book is mine.

tldr.rule: Subject (I/he) before verb. Object (me/him) after verb or preposition. Always 'between you and me', never 'and I'.
tldr.example: My friend and I went to the park. She gave gifts to my friend and me.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Bạn tôi và tôi đi công viên. Cô ấy tặng quà cho bạn tôi và tôi.

scenarios.why: After 'with', use object pronoun.
scenarios.english: Come with me to the cafe.
scenarios.vietnamese: Đi cà phê với tôi đi.

scenarios.why: Possessive adjective (my) before noun, possessive pronoun (mine) alone.
scenarios.english: That's my room. The room next door is hers.
scenarios.vietnamese: Đó là phòng của tôi. Phòng bên cạnh là của cô ấy.

scenarios.why: Reflexive pronoun emphasizes 'no help from anyone'.
scenarios.english: I built this website myself.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi tự xây trang web này một mình.

scenarios.why: 'Between you and me' — never 'between you and I'.
scenarios.english: This is between you and me.
scenarios.vietnamese: Chuyện này chỉ giữa bạn và tôi.

quickChecks.question: He went to the cafe with ___.
quickChecks.explanation: After preposition 'with' → object pronoun 'me'.

quickChecks.question: This book is ___.
quickChecks.explanation: No noun after it → possessive pronoun 'mine'.

quickChecks.question: She introduced ___ to the audience.
quickChecks.explanation: Subject and object are the same person → reflexive 'herself'.

quickChecks.question: ___ went to school.
quickChecks.explanation: Subject of the sentence → 'I' (and place yourself last for politeness).

sections.heading: Subject vs Object
sections.body: Subject pronoun starts the action. Object pronoun receives it.
bullets: Subject: I, you, he, she, it, we, they
Object: me, you, him, her, it, us, them
After every preposition → object form

sections.heading: Possessive adjective vs pronoun
sections.body: Adjective sits before a noun. Pronoun stands alone.
bullets: Adjective: my, your, his, her, its, our, their + noun
Pronoun: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs (alone)

sections.heading: Reflexive — when subject = object
sections.body: Use -self/-selves when the doer and receiver are the same person.

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt dùng 'tôi/của tôi' cho mọi vị trí. Tiếng Anh phân biệt rõ → sau giới từ luôn dùng me, không I.

commonMistakes.explanation: Subject → I, và đặt mình sau.

practice.vietnamese: Anh ấy đi với chúng tôi.
Đây là điện thoại của tôi.
Tôi tự làm nó.

lessons.title: Comparatives & Superlatives
lessons.description: Faster, fastest. More beautiful, most beautiful. Pick the right form by syllable count.

tldr.rule: Short adj → +er/+est. Long adj → more/most. Never combine: 'more taller' is wrong.
tldr.example: She runs faster than me, but he's the most consistent runner.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Cô ấy chạy nhanh hơn tôi, nhưng anh ấy chạy đều nhất.

scenarios.why: Comparing 2 things → comparative + 'than'.
scenarios.english: Lan is taller than Mai.
scenarios.vietnamese: Lan cao hơn Mai.

scenarios.context: 🏆 Picking the best of a group
scenarios.why: Comparing 3+ → superlative with 'the'.
scenarios.english: She is the smartest student in the class.
scenarios.vietnamese: Cô ấy là học sinh thông minh nhất lớp.

scenarios.why: Use comparative with 'than' for direct comparison.
scenarios.english: Coffee here is more expensive than at home.
scenarios.vietnamese: Cà phê ở đây đắt hơn ở nhà.

scenarios.why: Irregular: good → better → best. No 'more good'.
scenarios.english: This is the best film of the year.
scenarios.vietnamese: Đây là phim hay nhất năm.

quickChecks.question: She is ___ than me.
quickChecks.explanation: 1-syllable adj → +er. Never combine 'more' with '-er'.

quickChecks.question: This is ___ book I have ever read.
quickChecks.explanation: Superlative of 'good' is 'best', and we say 'the best'.

quickChecks.question: English is ___ than Vietnamese for me.
quickChecks.explanation: 'Difficult' is long (3 syllables) → use 'more'.

quickChecks.question: She is ___ in our group.
quickChecks.explanation: Comparing more than 2 people in a group → superlative.

sections.heading: Short adj — +er / +est
sections.body: 1-syllable and many 2-syllable adjectives.
bullets: Default: +er/+est (tall → taller → tallest)
Ends in -e: +r/+st (large → larger → largest)
Consonant + y: y → ier/iest (happy → happier → happiest)
CVC pattern: double the consonant (big → bigger → biggest)

sections.heading: Long adj — more / most
sections.body: 3+ syllables and most 2-syllable adjectives.

sections.heading: Irregulars — memorize
sections.body: These don't follow the pattern.
bullets: good → better → best
bad → worse → worst
far → farther/further → farthest/furthest
little → less → least
many/much → more → most

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt 'hơn'/'nhất' đứng SAU adj. Tiếng Anh thêm đuôi hoặc more/most TRƯỚC adj.
KHÔNG kết hợp 'more' với '-er': 'more taller' SAI.

commonMistakes.wrong: She is more taller than me.
This is the most good book.

commonMistakes.right: She is the most beautiful girl.

practice.vietnamese: Anh ấy nhanh hơn tôi.
Đây là cuốn sách hay nhất tôi từng đọc.
practice.english: This is the best book I have ever read.

practice.vietnamese: Tiếng Anh khó hơn tiếng Việt.
practice.english: English is more difficult than Vietnamese.

lessons.title: Modal Verbs: can, should, must, may
lessons.description: Modals add flavor: ability, obligation, advice, possibility. Mix them up and your sentence means something different.
lessons.rule: Modal + base verb (no 'to', no -s, no -ing). Choose by intent: ability (can), advice (should), obligation (must), possibility (may).

tldr.rule: Pick by intent: CAN (can do) · SHOULD (advice) · MUST (obligation) · MAY (possibility). Always + base verb.
tldr.example: You should see a doctor. You must rest. It may help.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Bạn nên đi khám. Bạn phải nghỉ ngơi. Có thể sẽ đỡ.

scenarios.why: 'Could you' is more polite than 'can you'.
scenarios.english: Could you help me with this?
scenarios.vietnamese: Bạn có thể giúp tôi việc này không?

scenarios.why: 'Should' is for suggestions and recommendations.
scenarios.english: You should drink more water.
scenarios.vietnamese: Bạn nên uống nhiều nước hơn.

scenarios.why: 'Must' for strong obligation (rules, laws).
scenarios.english: You must stop at red lights.
scenarios.vietnamese: Bạn phải dừng khi đèn đỏ.

scenarios.why: 'May/might' = possibility, not certainty.
scenarios.english: It might rain this afternoon.
scenarios.vietnamese: Chiều nay có thể mưa.

quickChecks.question: She ___ swim very well.
quickChecks.explanation: Modal verb: no -s, no 'to'. Just 'can swim'.

quickChecks.question: You ___ smoke in this building. It's against the rules.
quickChecks.explanation: Strict prohibition → 'mustn't'. 'Don't have to' means 'not necessary', different meaning.

quickChecks.question: You look tired. You ___ go to bed early.
quickChecks.explanation: Friendly advice → 'should'.

quickChecks.question: ___ I borrow your pen?
quickChecks.explanation: Polite request for permission → 'May I…?'

sections.heading: Can / Could — ability & permission
sections.body: Can = present ability. Could = past ability or polite request.

sections.heading: Must / Have to — obligation
sections.body: Must = internal sense of duty or strict rule. Have to = external obligation.
bullets: Mustn't = strictly forbidden (You mustn't smoke here)
Don't have to = not required (different from mustn't!)

sections.heading: Should — advice
sections.body: Friendly suggestion, weaker than must.

sections.heading: May / Might — possibility
sections.body: Something is possible but not certain.

examples.english: She might come to the party.

vietnameseTips: Modal verbs KHÔNG thêm -s, KHÔNG đi với 'to'. Sai phổ biến: 'She cans speak' → đúng 'She can speak'.
'Mustn't' (cấm) ≠ 'don't have to' (không cần). Phân biệt rõ.

commonMistakes.wrong: You don't have to smoke here.

practice.vietnamese: Bạn có thể giúp tôi mở cửa không?
practice.english: Could you help me open the door?

practice.vietnamese: Anh ấy nên đi ngủ sớm.
practice.english: He should go to bed early.

practice.vietnamese: Trời có thể mưa chiều nay.

lessons.title: Conditionals (if-clauses)
lessons.description: Real, possible, imaginary, regret — 4 types of 'if' for 4 different feelings.
lessons.rule: Each conditional pairs a tense in the if-clause with a tense in the result. Mix correctly or the meaning shifts.
examples: If water boils, it evaporates. (zero — fact)
If it rains, I will stay home. (first — likely)
If I were rich, I would travel. (second — imagined)
If I had studied, I would have passed. (third — past regret)

tldr.rule: Ask: how real is it? Fact → zero. Likely → first. Imagined now → second. Past regret → third.
tldr.example: If I were you, I would apologize. (you're not me, but imagining)
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Nếu tôi là bạn, tôi sẽ xin lỗi. (giả định)

scenarios.why: Likely future event → first conditional.
scenarios.english: If it rains tomorrow, I'll bring an umbrella.
scenarios.vietnamese: Nếu mai trời mưa, tôi sẽ mang ô.

scenarios.why: Imagining something not true → second conditional with 'were'.
scenarios.english: If I won the lottery, I would travel the world.
scenarios.vietnamese: Nếu tôi trúng số, tôi sẽ đi du lịch khắp thế giới.

scenarios.why: Past possibility that didn't happen → third conditional.
scenarios.english: If I had studied harder, I would have passed.
scenarios.vietnamese: Nếu tôi học chăm hơn, tôi đã đậu rồi.

scenarios.why: Always-true cause-effect → zero conditional, present tense both sides.
scenarios.english: If you heat ice, it melts.
scenarios.vietnamese: Nếu bạn đun đá, nó tan.

quickChecks.question: If it ___ tomorrow, I will stay home.
quickChecks.explanation: First conditional: if + present, result + will. Never 'will' in the if-clause.

quickChecks.question: If I ___ you, I would tell the truth.
quickChecks.explanation: Second conditional traditionally uses 'were' for all subjects (formal/standard).

quickChecks.question: If she had known, she ___ helped.
quickChecks.explanation: Third conditional: if + had + V3, result = would have + V3.

quickChecks.question: If you heat water to 100°C, it ___.
quickChecks.explanation: Zero conditional (universal fact): both sides in present simple.

sections.heading: Zero — facts
sections.body: If + present, present. For scientific facts and rules.

examples.english: If you heat ice, it melts.

sections.heading: First — likely future
sections.body: If + present, will + base. For things that may actually happen.
bullets: Never use 'will' in the if-clause
Can swap 'will' for can/may/should depending on certainty

sections.heading: Second — imagined now
sections.body: If + past, would + base. For unreal or unlikely present situations.

examples.english: If I were rich, I would buy a house.
examples.note: 'Were' for all subjects in formal English.

sections.heading: Third — regret about past
sections.body: If + had + V3, would have + V3. About what could have happened but didn't.

examples.english: If I had studied, I would have passed.

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt 'nếu… thì…' không phân biệt thực tế hay tưởng tượng — tiếng Anh chia tense.
Second/third conditional dùng 'were' (không 'was') cho mọi ngôi (chuẩn).

commonMistakes.wrong: If it will rain, I will stay home.
commonMistakes.right: If it rains, I will stay home.

commonMistakes.wrong: If I had studied, I would pass.
commonMistakes.right: If I had studied, I would have passed.

practice.vietnamese: Nếu trời mưa, tôi sẽ mang ô.
practice.english: If it rains, I will bring an umbrella.

practice.vietnamese: Nếu tôi là bạn, tôi sẽ học chăm hơn.
practice.english: If I were you, I would study harder.

practice.vietnamese: Nếu tôi biết, tôi đã giúp bạn rồi.
practice.english: If I had known, I would have helped you.

lessons.title: Passive Voice
lessons.description: Shift the focus from doer to receiver. Use it when WHO doesn't matter — or matters less than WHAT.
lessons.rule: Passive = be + V3 (past participle). The original object becomes the new subject.
examples: They build houses. → Houses are built.
She wrote the book. → The book was written by her.

tldr.rule: Use when the doer is unknown, obvious, or unimportant. Form: be (matching tense) + V3.
tldr.example: My wallet was stolen. (We don't know who.)
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Ví của tôi bị trộm. (Không biết ai.)

scenarios.why: Doer unknown → passive.
scenarios.english: My bike was stolen last night.
scenarios.vietnamese: Xe đạp của tôi bị trộm tối qua.

scenarios.why: Builder name is less important than the building itself.
scenarios.english: The Sydney Opera House was designed in 1957.
scenarios.vietnamese: Nhà hát Opera Sydney được thiết kế năm 1957.

scenarios.why: Subject is universal — saying everyone speaks English globally.
scenarios.english: English is spoken in over 50 countries.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tiếng Anh được nói ở hơn 50 quốc gia.

scenarios.why: Focus on the steps, not the workers.
scenarios.english: The package is delivered within 3 days.
scenarios.vietnamese: Hàng được giao trong 3 ngày.

quickChecks.question: The book ___ by Hemingway.
quickChecks.explanation: Past passive: was + V3 (written, not wrote).

quickChecks.question: My wallet ___ yesterday.
quickChecks.explanation: Past passive of 'steal' → was stolen.

quickChecks.question: The work ___ tomorrow.
quickChecks.explanation: Future passive: will be + V3.

quickChecks.question: English ___ everywhere.
quickChecks.explanation: Present passive: am/is/are + V3.

sections.heading: Forming passive — match the tense
sections.body: Conjugate 'be' to match the tense, then add V3.
bullets: Present: is/are + V3 → 'Coffee is grown in Brazil'
Past: was/were + V3 → 'The cake was eaten'
Present perfect: has/have been + V3
Future: will be + V3
Modal: modal + be + V3 → 'It must be done'

sections.heading: When to use passive
sections.body: Pick passive when the doer doesn't matter, is unknown, or is obvious.

sections.heading: Use 'by' only when relevant
sections.body: Mention the doer only if it adds meaning.

examples.english: The novel was written by Hemingway.

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt 'được/bị' không luôn = passive trong tiếng Anh. 'Tôi bị mất ví' = 'I lost my wallet' chứ không phải 'I was lost my wallet'.

commonMistakes.wrong: The book is wrote by him.
commonMistakes.right: The book is written by him.

commonMistakes.wrong: My wallet was stolen by someone.
commonMistakes.explanation: Tác nhân không cụ thể → bỏ 'by'.

commonMistakes.wrong: The work will be do tomorrow.
commonMistakes.right: The work will be done tomorrow.

practice.vietnamese: Cuốn sách này được viết năm 1990.
Tiếng Anh được dạy ở khắp nơi.
Dự án đã được hoàn thành.

lessons.title: Word Order
lessons.description: Subject-Verb-Object is fixed. Where do adverbs go? How do adjectives stack? Master the order so your English sounds natural.
lessons.rule: S+V+O. Frequency adverbs before main verb, after 'be'. Multiple adjectives follow OSASCOMP order.
examples: a beautiful big old red wooden table

tldr.rule: Adverbs of frequency before normal verb, after 'be'. Adjectives: opinion → size → age → color → origin → material → noun.
tldr.example: She is always late. I bought a beautiful small red Vietnamese basket.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Cô ấy luôn đến muộn. Tôi mua một cái giỏ Việt Nam nhỏ xinh màu đỏ.

scenarios.why: Frequency adverb (always/often/never) before the main verb.
scenarios.english: I always check my email first thing.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi luôn kiểm tra email đầu tiên.

scenarios.why: Stack adjectives in OSASCOMP order — opinion comes first.
scenarios.english: She wore a pretty long silk dress.
scenarios.vietnamese: Cô ấy mặc một cái váy lụa dài đẹp.

scenarios.why: Manner adverb after the object, not between verb and object.
scenarios.english: He speaks English fluently.
scenarios.vietnamese: Anh ấy nói tiếng Anh trôi chảy.

scenarios.why: Indirect + direct object order: give someone something.
scenarios.english: I gave my friend a book.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi tặng bạn tôi một quyển sách.

quickChecks.question: I ___ to the gym.
quickChecks.explanation: Frequency adverb 'always' goes BEFORE the main verb.

quickChecks.question: She speaks ___.
quickChecks.explanation: Manner adverb 'well' goes after the object 'English'.

quickChecks.question: I bought a ___ car.
quickChecks.explanation: Size comes before color in OSASCOMP order.

quickChecks.question: She is ___ late.
quickChecks.explanation: After 'be', frequency adverb comes RIGHT AFTER: 'She is never late'.

sections.heading: Basic word order
sections.body: Subject + Verb + Object is fixed. Vietnamese is more flexible, English is not.

examples.note: S + V + indirect O + direct O

sections.heading: Frequency adverbs
sections.body: Always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never.
bullets: Before the main verb: I always brush my teeth.
After 'be': She is never late.
After modals: We can usually finish on time.

sections.heading: Adjective order — OSASCOMP
sections.body: Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Color → Origin → Material → Purpose → Noun.

examples.english: a beautiful small old round red Vietnamese wooden cooking pot

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt: tính từ đứng SAU danh từ ('nhà to'). Tiếng Anh: TRƯỚC ('a big house').
Có nhiều tính từ? OSASCOMP order, hoặc đơn giản hơn: tách thành 2 câu.

commonMistakes.explanation: Trạng từ cách thức sau tân ngữ.

practice.vietnamese: Tôi luôn ăn sáng lúc 7 giờ.
Cô ấy là một cô gái Việt Nam xinh đẹp.
practice.english: She is a beautiful Vietnamese girl.

practice.vietnamese: Anh ấy nói tiếng Anh rất giỏi.

lessons.title: Relative Clauses
lessons.description: Who, which, that, whose, where — add information to a noun without starting a new sentence.
lessons.rule: Who = people. Which = things. That = either (in defining clauses only). Whose = possession. Where = place.
examples: The man who lives next door is friendly.
The book which I bought is interesting.
The city where I was born is Hanoi.

tldr.rule: Who for people, which for things, that for either. Commas → non-defining (extra info, can drop).
tldr.example: My brother, who lives in Hanoi, is a doctor.
tldr.exampleVietnamese: Anh tôi, sống ở Hà Nội, là bác sĩ.

scenarios.why: Add info about a person → 'who'.
scenarios.english: This is Lan, who works with me.
scenarios.vietnamese: Đây là Lan, đồng nghiệp tôi.

scenarios.why: Identify which specific book → defining clause, no commas.
scenarios.english: The book that I recommended is on the table.
scenarios.vietnamese: Cuốn sách tôi giới thiệu nằm trên bàn.

scenarios.why: Reference a place → 'where'.
scenarios.english: This is the town where I grew up.
scenarios.vietnamese: Đây là thị trấn tôi lớn lên.

scenarios.why: Possession → 'whose'.
scenarios.english: I have a friend whose mom is a chef.
scenarios.vietnamese: Tôi có một người bạn có mẹ là đầu bếp.

quickChecks.question: The book ___ I read was great.
quickChecks.explanation: Book is a thing → 'which' (or 'that').

quickChecks.question: The man ___ called me is my uncle.
quickChecks.explanation: Man is a person → 'who'.

quickChecks.question: This is the school ___ I studied.
quickChecks.explanation: Location → 'where'.

quickChecks.question: I met a woman ___ son is famous.
quickChecks.explanation: Possession (the woman's son) → 'whose'.

sections.heading: Defining vs Non-defining
sections.body: Defining = essential info, no commas. Non-defining = extra info, commas both sides.

examples.english: The man who called you is my friend.
My father, who lives in Hanoi, is a doctor.

bullets: Defining: no commas, 'that' is OK
Non-defining: commas both sides, NEVER 'that'

sections.heading: When to drop the pronoun
sections.body: If who/which/that is the OBJECT of a defining clause, you can drop it.

examples.english: The book (that) I bought is interesting.
The man who called me is here.

sections.heading: Whose & Where
sections.body: Special cases for possession and location.

examples.english: I met a woman whose son is famous.
This is the school where I studied.

vietnameseTips: Tiếng Việt dùng 'mà' cho cả người và vật. Tiếng Anh phân biệt who (người) vs which (vật).
'That' KHÔNG dùng trong non-defining clause (có dấu phẩy).

commonMistakes.wrong: My mother that lives in HCMC is a teacher.
commonMistakes.right: My mother, who lives in HCMC, is a teacher.

practice.vietnamese: Người phụ nữ đang ngồi đó là cô tôi.
practice.english: The woman who is sitting there is my aunt.

practice.vietnamese: Bộ phim tôi xem hôm qua rất hay.
practice.english: The movie (that) I watched yesterday was great.

practice.vietnamese: Đây là thành phố tôi lớn lên.
practice.english: This is the city where I grew up.
